summary: Energetic second set with Help‑Slipknot‑Franklin fire
blurb: The show bursts with a scorching Help On The Way → Slipknot! → Franklin’s Tower sequence and a soaring second‑set jam through Eyes of the World, The Other One and Spanish Jam. Jerry’s guitar is in top form while Phil lays down a cool bass line on Stella Blue. Overall a high‑energy performance that ranks among the strongest 1983 outings.
review: On October 20, 1983, the Dead opened a raucous night at The Centrum with a classic first set—Bertha, Greatest Story Ever Told and a tight Tennessee Jed—setting the stage for an unforgettable second act. The centerpiece was a smoking Help On The Way → Slipknot! → Franklin’s Tower suite that had the band “going bananas,” as reviewers noted, followed by a blistering run through Samson & Delilah, Eyes of the World and a marathon Space/Spanish Jam that flowed straight into a thunderous Other One. Brent’s keyboards shimmered, Mickey and Bill laid down a solid, jam‑focused rhythm foundation, and Phil’s bass turned “sooo cool” on Stella Blue despite a few vocal slips. The night closed with Good Lovin’ and the playful encore Keep Your Day Job, leaving fans citing this show as a high‑energy highlight of the 1983 tour.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four recordings are explicitly positive, praising the Help‑Slipknot‑Franklin sequence, second‑set jams, and Brent's keyboards; three recordings are mixed, noting minor vocal slips on Stella Blue but no serious performance flaws. The preponderance of enthusiastic language (“smoking,” “wildly inspired,” “soaring”) outweighs isolated criticisms, justifying a moderate positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Clear, well‑balanced sound with only a slight Jerry‑heavy mix; captures the energy of the show without dropouts.

key_highlights: Smoking Help On The Way → Slipknot! → Franklin’s Tower sequence
Powerful second‑set jam through Eyes Of The World, The Other One and Spanish Jam
Brent’s keyboard work shines as some of his best on the 1983 tour
High‑energy first set featuring Bertha, Greatest Story Ever Told and Tennessee Jed

band_performance.Jerry: Turns on the heat, especially on Help On The Way where he’s described as ‘going bananas,’ and delivers soaring vocals on Franklin’s Tower despite a minor slip on Stella Blue.
band_performance.Phil: Lays down a cool, “sooo” bass line throughout, adding intensity on Stella Blue and driving the groove during the extended jams.
band_performance.Bob: Adds energy with his classic ‘combine’ calls, nudging transitions forward and keeping the band tight.
band_performance.Brent: Provides richly textured keyboards that many reviewers call some of his best playing of the 1983 tour, especially in the second‑set jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Delivers a solid, jam‑focused foundation; while lacking his trademark “beam” moments, his drumming remains energetic and supportive.
band_performance.Billy: Locks in with Mickey to create a steady rhythmic base that fuels the high‑octane improvisations.
